args.content:
<!-- SCOPE
Covers: The final 10 letters of the alphabet, digraphs, apostrophe, and palatalization concepts.
Not covered:
  - Syllables and Word Division -> a1-05
-->

# The Cyrillic Code IV

> **Why does this matter?**
>
> You are at the absolute finish line! Це оста́ння части́на! (This is the final part!) You already know twenty-three letters of the Ukrainian alphabet. Now, we are going to learn the final ten letters, two special digraphs, and the apostrophe. Ми ма́ємо десять нови́х лі́тер. (We have ten new letters.) By the end of this lesson, you will have cracked the entire Cyrillic code and be able to read any Ukrainian word you see. Тепе́р ви мо́жете чита́ти! (Now you can read!)

## Вступ — Introduction

Приві́т! (Hello!) Welcome to the final stage of your alphabet journey! Це вели́ка перемога! (This is a big victory!) You already know twenty-three out of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet. You have mastered the familiar letters, the false friends that look like English but sound entirely different, and the unique characters from the previous lessons. Only ten letters, two double-letter sounds, and one special symbol remain to complete your knowledge. The finish line is completely in sight. 

Тест! (Test!) Before we learn the final pieces of the puzzle, let us do a quick diagnostic check. Can you read these five familiar words using the letters you have already learned? Чита́йте пома́лу. (Read slowly.)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ма́ма** | mom |
| **кіт** | cat |
| **дім** | house |
| **брат** | brother |
| **сестра́** | sister |

Ду́же до́бре! (Very good!) If you can read those words easily, you are perfectly ready for this final step. In this lesson, we will explain the grammar and mechanics in English, but you will see plenty of real Ukrainian examples to practice with. Почина́ємо! (We are starting!) Let us jump into the final set of letters and complete your alphabet once and for all!

## Злиті звуки та рідкісні букви — Affricates, Digraphs & Ґ

Now we will look at some unique consonants. Some of these represent blended sounds, while others are quite rare in the language. Learning these will help you recognize some of the most interesting words in Ukrainian. Це ціка́во! (This is interesting!) Два спеціа́льні зву́ки. (Two special sounds.)

### Літера Ц

Це лі́тера Ц. (This is the letter Ц.) The letter **Ц** represents an affricate. Це оди́н звук. (This is one sound.) This means it is a blend of the T and S sounds, pronounced together smoothly as one single sound. It sounds exactly like the "ts" at the end of the English word "cats" or "boots". It is crucial that you do not pronounce it as two completely separate consonants. Це ду́же важли́во запам'ята́ти. (This is very important to remember.)

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ц](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u44eCjR2Oz8)

You will see the letter **Ц** very often in word endings and right in the middle of words.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **це** | this is |
| **це́нтр** | center |

### Літера Щ

Це лі́тера Щ. (This is the letter Щ.) The letter **Щ** is uniquely Ukrainian and represents a fascinating sound. Це два зву́ки ра́зом. (These are two sounds together.) It represents two sounds crammed perfectly into one single letter: a firm SH sound followed immediately by a CH sound. This is a visual and phonetic trap! You must never confuse it with the letter **Ш**, which is just the plain, simple SH sound. 

Слу́хайте: (Listen:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Щ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QmBLieIuf6Q)

Listen carefully to the distinct double sound when you read these high-frequency words.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ще** | more, still |
| **ща́стя** | happiness |

> [!warning]
> Обере́жно! (Careful!) Be careful not to pronounce the letter **Щ** as a simple "sh" sound. It is always a distinct double sound: sh+ch. Saying the word **ще** should sound distinctly like "sh-che".

### Літера Ф

Це лі́тера Ф. (This is the letter Ф.) The letter **Ф** sounds exactly like the English F sound. It is the voiceless partner to the letter В. Interestingly, **Ф** is quite rare in native Ukrainian words. You will find it mostly in words that have been borrowed from other foreign languages, which actually makes them incredibly easy to recognize! Це ле́гко! (This is easy!)

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ф](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=haHRsFFZRQI)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **факт** | fact |
| **ка́фе** | cafe |

### Digraphs ДЖ and ДЗ

ДЖ і ДЗ — це дигра́фи. (ДЖ and ДЗ are digraphs.) A digraph is a pair of letters that combine to make one single unified sound. Ukrainian has two very important digraphs that you need to know. Вони́ познача́ють оди́н звук. (They represent one sound.)

The combination **ДЖ** sounds exactly like the English J sound you hear in words like "jungle" or "jump". Це оди́н звук. (This is one sound.)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **джаз** | jazz |

The combination **ДЗ** is a tight blend of D and Z into one single sound. There is no exact English equivalent, but you can think of the sound directly in the middle of the word "pads". Це украї́нський звук. (This is a Ukrainian sound.) This sound is uniquely Ukrainian.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **дзвін** | bell |

### Літера Ґ

Це лі́тера Ґ. (This is the letter Ґ.) The letter **Ґ** represents the hard G sound, exactly like the G in the English word "go" or "good". Вона́ ду́же рідкісна. (It is very rare.)

Слу́хайте ува́жно: (Listen carefully:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ґ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gNjHqjTW9WQ)

> [!history-bite]
> The letter **Ґ** is widely known as the repressed letter. It was officially removed from the Ukrainian alphabet by Soviet authorities in the year 1933 to artificially bring the Ukrainian language closer to Russian. It was proudly and officially restored to the alphabet in 1990! Це на́ша істо́рія. (This is our history.)

Because of this specific history, **Ґ** is extremely rare. There are only about four hundred native words that use it today. 

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ґа́нок** | porch |

## Йотовані голосні — Iotated Vowels Я, Ю, Є, Ї, Й

Я, Ю, Є, Ї — це йотова́ні голосні́. (Я, Ю, Є, Ї are iotated vowels.) Now we meet the fascinating "iotated" vowels. These specific letters are special because they closely follow a strict "dual nature" principle. At the very beginning of a word or directly after another vowel, they represent TWO separate sounds (a distinct Y-sound plus a vowel). Вони́ ма́ють два зву́ки. (They have two sounds.) However, when they come directly after a consonant, they represent ONE single sound and simply soften the consonant right before them. Але́ пі́сля при́голосного — оди́н звук. (But after a consonant — one sound.) Це пра́вило. (This is a rule.)

### Літера Я

Це лі́тера Я. (This is the letter Я.) The letter **Я** sounds exactly like "ya" (as in "yacht") when it starts a word. 

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Я](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yhSAf41LX8I)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **я** | I |
| **я́блуко** | apple |

When **Я** follows a consonant, it does not make the "y" sound at all. Instead, it softens the preceding consonant and just makes a simple "a" sound.

### Літера Ю

Це лі́тера Ю. (This is the letter Ю.) The letter **Ю** sounds exactly like "you" (as in "youth") when it begins a word.

Слу́хайте: (Listen:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ю](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9JdIBYCTWGw)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **юна́к** | young man |
| **суп-пюре́** | puree soup |

Just like the letter **Я**, when **Ю** follows a consonant, it immediately softens that consonant and simply makes an "oo" sound.

### Літера Є

Це лі́тера Є. (This is the letter Є.) The letter **Є** sounds exactly like "ye" (as in "yellow") at the beginning of a word.

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Є](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O0bwRyyBQSc)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **Євро́па** | Europe |

> [!culture]
> The letter **Є** is very important for a significant cultural touchstone. The concept of **Євро́па** (Europe) is central to modern Ukrainian identity and political direction, especially seen in powerful movements like the Euromaidan. Це Євро́па! (This is Europe!)

It is relatively rare to find **Є** immediately after a consonant, but when you do, it softens the consonant and makes a simple "e" sound.

### Літера Ї

Це лі́тера Ї. (This is the letter Ї.) The letter **Ї** is the strict exception to the dual nature rule. Це ви́няток. (This is an exception.) Вона́ за́вжди ма́є два зву́ки. (It always has two sounds.) It ALWAYS makes two distinct sounds: "yi" (as in "yield"). It NEVER softens a consonant under any circumstances. 

Слу́хайте: (Listen:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ї](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UcjdjQXhAY8)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ї́жа** | food |
| **Ки́їв** | Kyiv |

> [!fact]
> The letter **Ї** is beautifully and uniquely Ukrainian. During the full-scale invasion, drawing the letter **Ї** in occupied territories became a powerful, defiant symbol of Ukrainian resistance and national identity. Украї́на перемо́же! (Ukraine will win!)

### Літера Й

Це лі́тера Й. (This is the letter Й.) Це коро́ткий при́голосний. (This is a short consonant.) The letter **Й** is a very short, quick consonant sound. It sounds exactly like the "y" in the English words "boy" or "toy".

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Й](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aq0cjB90s3w)

Unlike the vowels we just learned, **Й** never forms a syllable on its own. You will find it very frequently at the absolute end of words or sitting comfortably right next to other vowels.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **чай** | tea |
| **мій** | my |

## М'який знак та апостроф — Soft Sign & Apostrophe

We have talked extensively about consonants becoming "soft" or palatalized in Ukrainian. Ukrainian actually has two special symbols that perfectly control this softening process: the soft sign and the apostrophe. І оди́н си́мвол. (And one symbol.) Це ду́же важли́во! (This is very important!)

### М'який знак (Ь)

Це м'яки́й знак. (This is the soft sign.) The soft sign **Ь** is not actually a letter with its own distinct sound. It is a powerful modifier. М'яки́й знак не ма́є зву́ку. (The soft sign has no sound.) Він пом'я́кшує при́голосний. (It softens the consonant.) Its only job in the entire language is to soften the consonant that comes directly before it.

Слу́хайте ува́жно: (Listen carefully:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ь](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cJlal8XKBxo)

Think of it as a silent, helpful partner that changes the quality of the preceding consonant, making it sound significantly lighter and closer to the roof of the mouth.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **день** | day |
| **Львів** | Lviv |

In the word **день**, the **Ь** smoothly makes the final N sound soft. In the famous city name **Львів**, the **Ь** effectively makes the L sound soft. 

### Апостроф (')

Це апо́строф. (This is an apostrophe.) The apostrophe serves the exact opposite purpose in Ukrainian writing. Апо́строф блоку́є пом'я́кшення. (The apostrophe blocks softening.) It acts as a strong blocker or a protective shield. It clearly separates a hard consonant from a following iotated vowel (**Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**). 

Why do we need this special shield? Because normally, an iotated vowel would automatically soften the consonant directly before it. The apostrophe completely prevents this softening from happening and forces the iotated vowel to stubbornly keep its full "y" sound. 

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **сім'я́** | family |
| **м'яч** | ball |

In the word **сім'я́**, the apostrophe strictly stops the **М** from becoming soft and perfectly ensures the **Я** is pronounced fully as "ya". 

> [!tip]
> Memorize this incredibly simple core rule: The soft sign (**Ь**) softens what comes BEFORE it. The apostrophe (**'**) BLOCKS softening and fiercely keeps the "y" sound in the following iotated vowel. Це пра́вило. (This is a rule.)

## Підсумок: Весь алфавіт — The Full Alphabet

Ось і все! (That is all!) Ви зна́єте весь алфаві́т! (You know the whole alphabet!) Чудо́во! (Excellent!) Congratulations on a huge achievement! You have now learned absolutely every single letter and special symbol needed to read Ukrainian. You have completely and successfully unlocked the Cyrillic code. 

Here is the complete thirty-three letter Ukrainian alphabet in its proper, traditional order:

**А - Б - В - Г - Ґ - Д - Е - Є - Ж - З - И - І - Ї - Й - К - Л - М - Н - О - П - Р - С - Т - У - Ф - Х - Ц - Ч - Ш - Щ - Ь - Ю - Я**

The entire alphabet is logically made up of ten standard vowels (which includes the four special iotated vowels), twenty-two consonants, and one special soft sign modifier. We also use two specific digraphs (ДЖ, ДЗ) and the apostrophe symbol to carefully help guide our pronunciation. 

You have confidently cracked the entire Cyrillic code today. Your next major step will be to learn about syllables and proper word division.

---

# Summary

You have successfully learned the final characters of the Ukrainian alphabet. You now completely know the affricates, the rare letters, the interesting dual nature of iotated vowels, and exactly how the soft sign and apostrophe deeply change pronunciation. You are finally ready to read absolutely any Ukrainian word you encounter!

- Який символ пом'якшує попередній приголосний? (Which symbol softens the preceding consonant?) — М'який знак (Ь) / The soft sign (Ь)
- Яка літера завжди позначає два звуки? (Which letter always makes two sounds?) — Ї / Ї
- Яка літера позначає звуки Ш та Ч разом? (Which letter represents the sounds SH and CH together?) — Щ / Щ
- Що робить апостроф? (What does the apostrophe do?) — Він блокує пом'якшення / It blocks softening

---

# The Cyrillic Code IV

> **Why does this matter?**

By the end of this lesson, you will have cracked the entire Cyrillic code and be able to read any Ukrainian word you see. Тепе́р ви мо́жете чита́ти! (Now you can read!)

## Вступ — Introduction

Приві́т! (Hello!) Welcome to the final stage of your alphabet journey! Це вели́ка перемога! (This is a big victory!) You already know twenty-three out of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet. You have mastered the familiar letters, the false friends that look like English but sound entirely different, and the unique characters from the previous lessons. Only ten letters, two double-letter sounds, and one special symbol remain to complete your knowledge. The finish line is completely in sight. 

Тест! (Test!) Before we learn the final pieces of the puzzle, let us do a quick diagnostic check. Can you read these five familiar words using the letters you have already learned? Чита́йте пома́лу. (Read slowly.)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ма́ма** | mom |
| **кіт** | cat |
...

Ду́же до́бре! (Very good!) If you can read those words easily, you are perfectly ready for this final step. In this lesson, we will explain the grammar and mechanics in English, but you will see plenty of real Ukrainian examples to practice with. Почина́ємо! (We are starting!) Let us jump into the final set of letters and complete your alphabet once and for all!

## Злиті звуки та рідкісні букви — Affricates, Digraphs & Ґ

Now we will look at some unique consonants. Some of these represent blended sounds, while others are quite rare in the language. Learning these will help you recognize some of the most interesting words in Ukrainian. Це ціка́во! (This is interesting!) Два спеціа́льні зву́ки. (Two special sounds.)

### Літера Ц

Це лі́тера Ц. (This is the letter Ц.) The letter **Ц** represents an affricate. Це оди́н звук. (This is one sound.) This means it is a blend of the T and S sounds, pronounced together smoothly as one single sound. It sounds exactly like the "ts" at the end of the English word "cats" or "boots". It is crucial that you do not pronounce it as two completely separate consonants. Це ду́же важли́во запам'ята́ти. (This is very important to remember.)

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ц](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u44eCjR2Oz8)

You will see the letter **Ц** very often in word endings and right in the middle of words.

...
| **це** | this is |
| **це́нтр** | center |

### Літера Щ

Це лі́тера Щ. (This is the letter Щ.) The letter **Щ** is uniquely Ukrainian and represents a fascinating sound. Це два зву́ки ра́зом. (These are two sounds together.) It represents two sounds crammed perfectly into one single letter: a firm SH sound followed immediately by a CH sound. This is a visual and phonetic trap! You must never confuse it with the letter **Ш**, which is just the plain, simple SH sound. 

Слу́хайте: (Listen:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Щ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QmBLieIuf6Q)

Listen carefully to the distinct double sound when you read these high-frequency words.

...
| **факт** | fact |
| **ка́фе** | cafe |

### Digraphs ДЖ and ДЗ

ДЖ і ДЗ — це дигра́фи. (ДЖ and ДЗ are digraphs.) A digraph is a pair of letters that combine to make one single unified sound. Ukrainian has two very important digraphs that you need to know. Вони́ познача́ють оди́н звук. (They represent one sound.)

The combination **ДЖ** sounds exactly like the English J sound you hear in words like "jungle" or "jump". Це оди́н звук. (This is one sound.)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
...
|------|---------|
| **дзвін** | bell |

### Літера Ґ

Це лі́тера Ґ. (This is the letter Ґ.) The letter **Ґ** represents the hard G sound, exactly like the G in the English word "go" or "good". Вона́ ду́же рідкісна. (It is very rare.)

Слу́хайте ува́жно: (Listen carefully:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ґ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gNjHqjTW9WQ)

> [!history-bite]
> The letter **Ґ** is widely known as the repressed letter. It was officially removed from the Ukrainian alphabet by Soviet authorities in the year 1933 to artificially bring the Ukrainian language closer to Russian. It was proudly and officially restored to the alphabet in 1990! Це на́ша істо́рія. (This is our history.)
...
|------|---------|
| **ґа́нок** | porch |

## Йотовані голосні — Iotated Vowels Я, Ю, Є, Ї, Й

Я, Ю, Є, Ї — це йотова́ні голосні́. (Я, Ю, Є, Ї are iotated vowels.) Now we meet the fascinating "iotated" vowels. These specific letters are special because they closely follow a strict "dual nature" principle. At the very beginning of a word or directly after another vowel, they represent TWO separate sounds (a distinct Y-sound plus a vowel). Вони́ ма́ють два зву́ки. (They have two sounds.) However, when they come directly after a consonant, they represent ONE single sound and simply soften the consonant right before them. Але́ пі́сля при́голосного — оди́н звук. (But after a consonant — one sound.) Це пра́вило. (This is a rule.)

### Літера Я

Це лі́тера Я. (This is the letter Я.) The letter **Я** sounds exactly like "ya" (as in "yacht") when it starts a word. 

...

It is relatively rare to find **Є** immediately after a consonant, but when you do, it softens the consonant and makes a simple "e" sound.

### Літера Ї

Це лі́тера Ї. (This is the letter Ї.) The letter **Ї** is the strict exception to the dual nature rule. Це ви́няток. (This is an exception.) Вона́ за́вжди ма́є два зву́ки. (It always has two sounds.) It ALWAYS makes two distinct sounds: "yi" (as in "yield"). It NEVER softens a consonant under any circumstances. 

Слу́хайте: (Listen:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ї](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UcjdjQXhAY8)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
...
> [!fact]

Це лі́тера Й. (This is the letter Й.) Це коро́ткий при́голосний. (This is a short consonant.) The letter **Й** is a very short, quick consonant sound. It sounds exactly like the "y" in the English words "boy" or "toy".

+Ось і все! (That is all!) Ви зна́єте весь алфаві́т! (You know the whole alphabet!) Чудо́во! (Excellent!) Congratulations on a huge achievement! You have now learned absolutely every single letter and special symbol needed to read Ukrainian. You have completely and successfully unlocked the Cyrillic code. 
 
 Here is the complete thirty-three letter Ukrainian alphabet in its proper, traditional order:
 
-**А Б В Г Ґ Д Е Є Ж З И І Ї Й К Л М Н О П Р С Т У Ф Х Ц Ч Ш Щ Ь Ю Я**
+**А - Б - В - Г - Ґ - Д - Е - Є - Ж - З - И - І - Ї - Й - К - Л - М - Н - О - П - Р - С - Т - У - Ф - Х - Ц - Ч - Ш - Щ - Ь - Ю - Я**
 
 The entire alphabet is logically made up of ten standard vowels (which includes the four special iotated vowels), twenty-two consonants, and one special soft sign modifier. We also use two specific digraphs (ДЖ, ДЗ) and the apostrophe symbol to carefully help guide our pronunciation.

# The Cyrillic Code IV

> **Why does this matter?**
>
> You are at the absolute finish line! You already know twenty-three letters of the Ukrainian alphabet. Now, we are going to learn the final ten letters, two special digraphs, and the apostrophe. By the end of this lesson, you will have cracked the entire Cyrillic code and be able to read any Ukrainian word you see.

## Вступ — Introduction

Приві́т! (Hello!) Welcome to the final stage of your alphabet journey! Це вели́ка перемога! (This is a big victory!) You already know twenty-three out of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet. You have mastered the familiar letters, the false friends that look like English but sound entirely different, and the unique characters from the previous lessons. Only ten letters, two double-letter sounds, and one special symbol remain to complete your knowledge. The finish line is completely in sight. 

Переві́рка! (Check!) Before we learn the final pieces of the puzzle, let us do a quick diagnostic check. Can you read these five familiar words using the letters you have already learned? Чита́йте пома́лу. (Read slowly.)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ма́ма** | mom |
| **кіт** | cat |
| **дім** | house |
| **брат** | brother |
| **сестра́** | sister |

Ду́же до́бре! (Very good!) If you can read those words easily, you are perfectly ready for this final step. In this lesson, we will explain the grammar and mechanics in English, but you will see plenty of real Ukrainian examples to practice with. Почина́ємо! (We are starting!) Let us jump into the final set of letters and complete your alphabet once and for all!

## Злиті звуки та рідкісні букви — Affricates, Digraphs & Ґ

Now we will look at some unique consonants. Some of these represent blended sounds, while others are quite rare in the language. Learning these will help you recognize some of the most interesting words in Ukrainian. Це ціка́во! (This is interesting!)

### Літера Ц

Це лі́тера Ц. (This is the letter Ц.) The letter **Ц** represents an affricate. Це оди́н звук. (This is one sound.) This means it is a blend of the T and S sounds, pronounced together smoothly as one single sound. It sounds exactly like the "ts" at the end of the English word "cats" or "boots". It is crucial that you do not pronounce it as two completely separate consonants. 

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ц](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u44eCjR2Oz8)

You will see the letter **Ц** very often in word endings and right in the middle of words.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **це** | this is |
| **це́нтр** | center |

### Літера Щ

Це лі́тера Щ. (This is the letter Щ.) The letter **Щ** is uniquely Ukrainian and represents a fascinating sound. It represents two sounds crammed perfectly into one single letter: a firm SH sound followed immediately by a CH sound. This is a visual and phonetic trap! You must never confuse it with the letter **Ш**, which is just the plain, simple SH sound. 

Слу́хайте: (Listen:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Щ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QmBLieIuf6Q)

Listen carefully to the distinct double sound when you read these high-frequency words.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ще** | more, still |
| **ща́стя** | happiness |

> [!warning]
> Обере́жно! (Careful!) Be careful not to pronounce the letter **Щ** as a simple "sh" sound. It is always a distinct double sound: sh+ch. Saying the word **ще** should sound distinctly like "sh-che".

### Літера Ф

Це лі́тера Ф. (This is the letter Ф.) The letter **Ф** sounds exactly like the English F sound. It is the voiceless partner to the letter В. Interestingly, **Ф** is quite rare in native Ukrainian words. You will find it mostly in words that have been borrowed from other foreign languages, which actually makes them incredibly easy to recognize! Це ле́гко! (This is easy!)

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ф](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=haHRsFFZRQI)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **факт** | fact |
| **ка́фе** | cafe |

### Digraphs ДЖ and ДЗ

A digraph is a pair of letters that combine to make one single unified sound. Ukrainian has two very important digraphs that you need to know.

The combination **ДЖ** sounds exactly like the English J sound you hear in words like "jungle" or "jump". Це оди́н звук. (This is one sound.)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **джаз** | jazz |

The combination **ДЗ** is a tight blend of D and Z into one single sound. There is no exact English equivalent, but you can think of the sound directly in the middle of the word "pads". Це украї́нський звук. (This is a Ukrainian sound.) This sound is uniquely Ukrainian.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **дзвін** | bell |

### Літера Ґ

Це лі́тера Ґ. (This is the letter Ґ.) The letter **Ґ** represents the hard G sound, exactly like the G in the English word "go" or "good". 

Слу́хайте ува́жно: (Listen carefully:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ґ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gNjHqjTW9WQ)

> [!history-bite]
> The letter **Ґ** is widely known as the repressed letter. It was officially removed from the Ukrainian alphabet by Soviet authorities in the year 1933 to artificially bring the Ukrainian language closer to Russian. It was proudly and officially restored to the alphabet in 1990! Це на́ша істо́рія. (This is our history.)

Because of this specific history, **Ґ** is extremely rare. There are only about four hundred native words that use it today. 

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ґа́нок** | porch |

## Йотовані голосні — Iotated Vowels Я, Ю, Є, Ї, Й

Now we meet the fascinating "iotated" vowels. These specific letters are special because they closely follow a strict "dual nature" principle. At the very beginning of a word or directly after another vowel, they represent TWO separate sounds (a distinct Y-sound plus a vowel). However, when they come directly after a consonant, they represent ONE single sound and simply soften the consonant right before them. Це пра́вило. (This is a rule.)

### Літера Я

Це лі́тера Я. (This is the letter Я.) The letter **Я** sounds exactly like "ya" (as in "yacht") when it starts a word. 

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Я](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yhSAf41LX8I)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **я** | I |
| **я́блуко** | apple |

When **Я** follows a consonant, it does not make the "y" sound at all. Instead, it softens the preceding consonant and just makes a simple "a" sound.

### Літера Ю

Це лі́тера Ю. (This is the letter Ю.) The letter **Ю** sounds exactly like "you" (as in "youth") when it begins a word.

Слу́хайте: (Listen:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ю](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9JdIBYCTWGw)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **юна́к** | young man |
| **суп-пюре́** | puree soup |

Just like the letter **Я**, when **Ю** follows a consonant, it immediately softens that consonant and simply makes an "oo" sound.

### Літера Є

Це лі́тера Є. (This is the letter Є.) The letter **Є** sounds exactly like "ye" (as in "yellow") at the beginning of a word.

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Є](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O0bwRyyBQSc)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **Євро́па** | Europe |

> [!culture]
> The letter **Є** is very important for a significant cultural touchstone. The concept of **Євро́па** (Europe) is central to modern Ukrainian identity and political direction, especially seen in powerful movements like the Euromaidan. Це Євро́па! (This is Europe!)

It is relatively rare to find **Є** immediately after a consonant, but when you do, it softens the consonant and makes a simple "e" sound.

### Літера Ї

Це лі́тера Ї. (This is the letter Ї.) The letter **Ї** is the strict exception to the dual nature rule. Це ви́няток. (This is an exception.) It ALWAYS makes two distinct sounds: "yi" (as in "yield"). It NEVER softens a consonant under any circumstances. 

Слу́хайте: (Listen:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ї](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UcjdjQXhAY8)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ї́жа** | food |
| **Ки́їв** | Kyiv |

> [!fact]
> The letter **Ї** is beautifully and uniquely Ukrainian. During the full-scale invasion, drawing the letter **Ї** in occupied territories became a powerful, defiant symbol of Ukrainian resistance and national identity. Украї́на перемо́же! (Ukraine will win!)

### Літера Й

Це лі́тера Й. (This is the letter Й.) The letter **Й** is a very short, quick consonant sound. It sounds exactly like the "y" in the English words "boy" or "toy".

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Й](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aq0cjB90s3w)

Unlike the vowels we just learned, **Й** never forms a syllable on its own. You will find it very frequently at the absolute end of words or sitting comfortably right next to other vowels.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **чай** | tea |
| **мій** | my |

## М'який знак та апостроф — Soft Sign & Apostrophe

We have talked extensively about consonants becoming "soft" or palatalized in Ukrainian. Ukrainian actually has two special symbols that perfectly control this softening process: the soft sign and the apostrophe. Це ду́же важли́во! (This is very important!)

### М'який знак (Ь)

Це м'яки́й знак. (This is the soft sign.) The soft sign **Ь** is not actually a letter with its own distinct sound. It is a powerful modifier. Its only job in the entire language is to soften the consonant that comes directly before it.

Слу́хайте ува́жно: (Listen carefully:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ь](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cJlal8XKBxo)

Think of it as a silent, helpful partner that changes the quality of the preceding consonant, making it sound significantly lighter and closer to the roof of the mouth.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **день** | day |
| **Львів** | Lviv |

In the word **день**, the **Ь** smoothly makes the final N sound soft. In the famous city name **Львів**, the **Ь** effectively makes the L sound soft. 

### Апостроф (')

Це апо́строф. (This is an apostrophe.) The apostrophe serves the exact opposite purpose in Ukrainian writing. It acts as a strong blocker or a protective shield. It clearly separates a hard consonant from a following iotated vowel (**Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**). 

Why do we need this special shield? Because normally, an iotated vowel would automatically soften the consonant directly before it. The apostrophe completely prevents this softening from happening and forces the iotated vowel to stubbornly keep its full "y" sound. 

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **сім'я́** | family |
| **м'яч** | ball |

In the word **сім'я́**, the apostrophe strictly stops the **М** from becoming soft and perfectly ensures the **Я** is pronounced fully as "ya". 

> [!tip]
> Memorize this incredibly simple core rule: The soft sign (**Ь**) softens what comes BEFORE it. The apostrophe (**'**) BLOCKS softening and fiercely keeps the "y" sound in the following iotated vowel. Це пра́вило. (This is a rule.)

## Підсумок: Весь алфавіт — The Full Alphabet

Чудо́во! (Excellent!) Congratulations on a huge achievement! You have now learned absolutely every single letter and special symbol needed to read Ukrainian. You have completely and successfully unlocked the Cyrillic code. 

Here is the complete thirty-three letter Ukrainian alphabet in its proper, traditional order:

**А Б В Г Ґ Д Е Є Ж З И І Ї Й К Л М Н О П Р С Т У Ф Х Ц Ч Ш Щ Ь Ю Я**

The entire alphabet is logically made up of ten standard vowels (which includes the four special iotated vowels), twenty-two consonants, and one special soft sign modifier. We also use two specific digraphs (ДЖ, ДЗ) and the apostrophe symbol to carefully help guide our pronunciation. 

You have confidently cracked the entire Cyrillic code today. Your next major step will be to learn about syllables and proper word division.

---

# Summary

You have successfully learned the final characters of the Ukrainian alphabet. You now completely know the affricates, the rare letters, the interesting dual nature of iotated vowels, and exactly how the soft sign and apostrophe deeply change pronunciation. You are finally ready to read absolutely any Ukrainian word you encounter!

- Який символ пом'якшує попередній приголосний? (Which symbol softens the preceding consonant?) — М'який знак (Ь) / The soft sign (Ь)
- Яка літера завжди позначає два звуки? (Which letter always makes two sounds?) — Ї / Ї
- Яка літера позначає звуки Ш та Ч разом? (Which letter represents the sounds SH and CH together?) — Щ / Щ
- Що робить апостроф? (What does the apostrophe do?) — Він блокує пом'якшення / It blocks softening

---

| **чай** | tea |
| **мій** | my |

## М'який знак та апостроф — Soft Sign & Apostrophe

We have talked extensively about consonants becoming "soft" or palatalized in Ukrainian. Ukrainian actually has two special symbols that perfectly control this softening process: the soft sign and the apostrophe. І оди́н си́мвол. (And one symbol.) Це ду́же важли́во! (This is very important!)

### М'який знак (Ь)

Це м'яки́й знак. (This is the soft sign.) The soft sign **Ь** is not actually a letter with its own distinct sound. It is a powerful modifier. М'яки́й знак не ма́є зву́ку. (The soft sign has no sound.) Він пом'я́кшує при́голосний. (It softens the consonant.) Its only job in the entire language is to soften the consonant that comes directly before it.

Слу́хайте ува́жно: (Listen carefully:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ь](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cJlal8XKBxo)

Think of it as a silent, helpful partner that changes the quality of the preceding consonant, making it sound significantly lighter and closer to the roof of the mouth.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **день** | day |
| **Львів** | Lviv |

In the word **день**, the **Ь** smoothly makes the final N sound soft. In the famous city name **Львів**, the **Ь** effectively makes the L sound soft. 

### Апостроф (')

Це апо́строф. (This is an apostrophe.) The apostrophe serves the exact opposite purpose in Ukrainian writing. Апо́строф блоку́є пом'я́кшення. (The apostrophe blocks softening.) It acts as a strong blocker or a protective shield. It clearly separates a hard consonant from a following iotated vowel (**Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**). 

Why do we need this special shield? Because normally, an iotated vowel would automatically soften the consonant directly before it. The apostrophe completely prevents this softening from happening and forces the iotated vowel to stubbornly keep its full "y" sound. 

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **сім'я́** | family |
| **м'яч** | ball |

In the word **сім'я́**, the apostrophe strictly stops the **М** from becoming soft and perfectly ensures the **Я** is pronounced fully as "ya". 

> [!tip]
> Memorize this incredibly simple core rule: The soft sign (**Ь**) softens what comes BEFORE it. The apostrophe (**'**) BLOCKS softening and fiercely keeps the "y" sound in the following iotated vowel. Це пра́вило. (This is a rule.)

## Підсумок: Весь алфавіт — The Full Alphabet

Ось і все! (That is all!) Ви зна́єте весь алфаві́т! (You know the whole alphabet!) Чудо́во! (Excellent!) Congratulations on a huge achievement! You have now learned absolutely every single letter and special symbol needed to read Ukrainian. You have completely and successfully unlocked the Cyrillic code. 

Here is the complete thirty-three letter Ukrainian alphabet in its proper, traditional order:

**А - Б - В - Г - Ґ - Д - Е - Є - Ж - З - И - І - Ї - Й - К - Л - М - Н - О - П - Р - С - Т - У - Ф - Х - Ц - Ч - Ш - Щ - Ь - Ю - Я**

The entire alphabet is logically made up of ten standard vowels (which includes the four special iotated vowels), twenty-two consonants, and one special soft sign modifier. We also use two specific digraphs (ДЖ, ДЗ) and the apostrophe symbol to carefully help guide our pronunciation. 

You have confidently cracked the entire Cyrillic code today. Your next major step will be to learn about syllables and proper word division.

---

# Summary

You have successfully learned the final characters of the Ukrainian alphabet. You now completely know the affricates, the rare letters, the interesting dual nature of iotated vowels, and exactly how the soft sign and apostrophe deeply change pronunciation. You are finally ready to read absolutely any Ukrainian word you encounter!

- Який символ пом'якшує попередній приголосний? (Which symbol softens the preceding consonant?) — М'який знак (Ь) / The soft sign (Ь)
- Яка літера завжди позначає два звуки? (Which letter always makes two sounds?) — Ї / Ї
- Яка літера позначає звуки Ш та Ч разом? (Which letter represents the sounds SH and CH together?) — Щ / Щ
- Що робить апостроф? (What does the apostrophe do?) — Він блокує пом'якшення / It blocks softening

---

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Efficiency Guidelines:
      - Quiet Flags: Always prefer silent or quiet flags (e.g., `npm install --silent`, `git --no-pager`) to reduce output volume while still capturing necessary information.
      - Pagination: Always disable terminal pagination to ensure commands terminate (e.g., use `git --no-pager`, `systemctl --no-pager`, or set `PAGER=cat`).

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

messages.content:
===CONTENT_START===

<!-- SCOPE
Covers: The final 10 letters of the alphabet, digraphs, apostrophe, and palatalization concepts.
Not covered:
  - Syllables and Word Division -> a1-05
-->

# The Cyrillic Code IV

> **Why does this matter?**
>
> You are at the absolute finish line! Це оста́ння части́на! (This is the final part!) You already know twenty-three letters of the Ukrainian alphabet. Now, we are going to learn the final ten letters, two special digraphs, and the apostrophe. Ми ма́ємо десять нови́х лі́тер. (We have ten new letters.) By the end of this lesson, you will have cracked the entire Cyrillic code and be able to read any Ukrainian word you see. Тепе́р ви мо́жете чита́ти! (Now you can read!)

## Вступ — Introduction

Приві́т! (Hello!) Welcome to the final stage of your alphabet journey! Це вели́ка перемога! (This is a big victory!) You already know twenty-three out of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet. You have mastered the familiar letters, the false friends that look like English but sound entirely different, and the unique characters from the previous lessons. Only ten letters, two double-letter sounds, and one special symbol remain to complete your knowledge. The finish line is completely in sight. 

Тест! (Test!) Before we learn the final pieces of the puzzle, let us do a quick diagnostic check. Can you read these five familiar words using the letters you have already learned? Чита́йте пома́лу. (Read slowly.)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ма́ма** | mom |
| **кіт** | cat |
| **дім** | house |
| **брат** | brother |
| **сестра́** | sister |

Ду́же до́бре! (Very good!) If you can read those words easily, you are perfectly ready for this final step. In this lesson, we will explain the grammar and mechanics in English, but you will see plenty of real Ukrainian examples to practice with. Почина́ємо! (We are starting!) Let us jump into the final set of letters and complete your alphabet once and for all!

## Злиті звуки та рідкісні букви — Affricates, Digraphs & Ґ

Now we will look at some unique consonants. Some of these represent blended sounds, while others are quite rare in the language. Learning these will help you recognize some of the most interesting words in Ukrainian. Це ціка́во! (This is interesting!) Два спеціа́льні зву́ки. (Two special sounds.)

### Літера Ц

Це лі́тера Ц. (This is the letter Ц.) The letter **Ц** represents an affricate. Це оди́н звук. (This is one sound.) This means it is a blend of the T and S sounds, pronounced together smoothly as one single sound. It sounds exactly like the "ts" at the end of the English word "cats" or "boots". It is crucial that you do not pronounce it as two completely separate consonants. Це ду́же важли́во запам'ята́ти. (This is very important to remember.)

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ц](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u44eCjR2Oz8)

You will see the letter **Ц** very often in word endings and right in the middle of words.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **це** | this is |
| **це́нтр** | center |

### Літера Щ

Це лі́тера Щ. (This is the letter Щ.) The letter **Щ** is uniquely Ukrainian and represents a fascinating sound. Це два зву́ки ра́зом. (These are two sounds together.) It represents two sounds crammed perfectly into one single letter: a firm SH sound followed immediately by a CH sound. This is a visual and phonetic trap! You must never confuse it with the letter **Ш**, which is just the plain, simple SH sound. 

Слу́хайте: (Listen:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Щ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QmBLieIuf6Q)

Listen carefully to the distinct double sound when you read these high-frequency words.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ще** | more, still |
| **ща́стя** | happiness |

> [!warning]
> Обере́жно! (Careful!) Be careful not to pronounce the letter **Щ** as a simple "sh" sound. It is always a distinct double sound: sh+ch. Saying the word **ще** should sound distinctly like "sh-che".

### Літера Ф

Це лі́тера Ф. (This is the letter Ф.) The letter **Ф** sounds exactly like the English F sound. It is the voiceless partner to the letter В. Interestingly, **Ф** is quite rare in native Ukrainian words. You will find it mostly in words that have been borrowed from other foreign languages, which actually makes them incredibly easy to recognize! Це ле́гко! (This is easy!)

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ф](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=haHRsFFZRQI)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **факт** | fact |
| **ка́фе** | cafe |

### Digraphs ДЖ and ДЗ

ДЖ і ДЗ — це дигра́фи. (ДЖ and ДЗ are digraphs.) A digraph is a pair of letters that combine to make one single unified sound. Ukrainian has two very important digraphs that you need to know. Вони́ познача́ють оди́н звук. (They represent one sound.)

The combination **ДЖ** sounds exactly like the English J sound you hear in words like "jungle" or "jump". Це оди́н звук. (This is one sound.)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **джаз** | jazz |

The combination **ДЗ** is a tight blend of D and Z into one single sound. There is no exact English equivalent, but you can think of the sound directly in the middle of the word "pads". Це украї́нський звук. (This is a Ukrainian sound.) This sound is uniquely Ukrainian.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **дзвін** | bell |

### Літера Ґ

Це лі́тера Ґ. (This is the letter Ґ.) The letter **Ґ** represents the hard G sound, exactly like the G in the English word "go" or "good". Вона́ ду́же рідкісна. (It is very rare.)

Слу́хайте ува́жно: (Listen carefully:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ґ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gNjHqjTW9WQ)

> [!history-bite]
> The letter **Ґ** is widely known as the repressed letter. It was officially removed from the Ukrainian alphabet by Soviet authorities in the year 1933 to artificially bring the Ukrainian language closer to Russian. It was proudly and officially restored to the alphabet in 1990! Це на́ша істо́рія. (This is our history.)

Because of this specific history, **Ґ** is extremely rare. There are only about four hundred native words that use it today. 

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ґа́нок** | porch |

## Йотовані голосні — Iotated Vowels Я, Ю, Є, Ї, Й

Я, Ю, Є, Ї — це йотова́ні голосні́. (Я, Ю, Є, Ї are iotated vowels.) Now we meet the fascinating "iotated" vowels. These specific letters are special because they closely follow a strict "dual nature" principle. At the very beginning of a word or directly after another vowel, they represent TWO separate sounds (a distinct Y-sound plus a vowel). Вони́ ма́ють два зву́ки. (They have two sounds.) However, when they come directly after a consonant, they represent ONE single sound and simply soften the consonant right before them. Але́ пі́сля при́голосного — оди́н звук. (But after a consonant — one sound.) Це пра́вило. (This is a rule.)

### Літера Я

Це лі́тера Я. (This is the letter Я.) The letter **Я** sounds exactly like "ya" (as in "yacht") when it starts a word. 

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Я](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yhSAf41LX8I)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **я** | I |
| **я́блуко** | apple |

When **Я** follows a consonant, it does not make the "y" sound at all. Instead, it softens the preceding consonant and just makes a simple "a" sound.

### Літера Ю

Це лі́тера Ю. (This is the letter Ю.) The letter **Ю** sounds exactly like "you" (as in "youth") when it begins a word.

Слу́хайте: (Listen:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ю](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9JdIBYCTWGw)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **юна́к** | young man |
| **суп-пюре́** | puree soup |

Just like the letter **Я**, when **Ю** follows a consonant, it immediately softens that consonant and simply makes an "oo" sound.

### Літера Є

Це лі́тера Є. (This is the letter Є.) The letter **Є** sounds exactly like "ye" (as in "yellow") at the beginning of a word.

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Є](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O0bwRyyBQSc)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **Євро́па** | Europe |

> [!culture]
> The letter **Є** is very important for a significant cultural touchstone. The concept of **Євро́па** (Europe) is central to modern Ukrainian identity and political direction, especially seen in powerful movements like the Euromaidan. Це Євро́па! (This is Europe!)

It is relatively rare to find **Є** immediately after a consonant, but when you do, it softens the consonant and makes a simple "e" sound.

### Літера Ї

Це лі́тера Ї. (This is the letter Ї.) The letter **Ї** is the strict exception to the dual nature rule. Це ви́няток. (This is an exception.) Вона́ за́вжди ма́є два зву́ки. (It always has two sounds.) It ALWAYS makes two distinct sounds: "yi" (as in "yield"). It NEVER softens a consonant under any circumstances. 

Слу́хайте: (Listen:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ї](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UcjdjQXhAY8)

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **ї́жа** | food |
| **Ки́їв** | Kyiv |

> [!fact]
> The letter **Ї** is beautifully and uniquely Ukrainian. During the full-scale invasion, drawing the letter **Ї** in occupied territories became a powerful, defiant symbol of Ukrainian resistance and national identity. Украї́на перемо́же! (Ukraine will win!)

### Літера Й

Це лі́тера Й. (This is the letter Й.) Це коро́ткий при́голосний. (This is a short consonant.) The letter **Й** is a very short, quick consonant sound. It sounds exactly like the "y" in the English words "boy" or "toy".

Диві́ться ві́део: (Watch the video:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Й](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aq0cjB90s3w)

Unlike the vowels we just learned, **Й** never forms a syllable on its own. You will find it very frequently at the absolute end of words or sitting comfortably right next to other vowels.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **чай** | tea |
| **мій** | my |

## М'який знак та апостроф — Soft Sign & Apostrophe

We have talked extensively about consonants becoming "soft" or palatalized in Ukrainian. Ukrainian actually has two special symbols that perfectly control this softening process: the soft sign and the apostrophe. І оди́н си́мвол. (And one symbol.) Це ду́же важли́во! (This is very important!)

### М'який знак (Ь)

Це м'яки́й знак. (This is the soft sign.) The soft sign **Ь** is not actually a letter with its own distinct sound. It is a powerful modifier. М'яки́й знак не ма́є зву́ку. (The soft sign has no sound.) Він пом'я́кшує при́голосний. (It softens the consonant.) Its only job in the entire language is to soften the consonant that comes directly before it.

Слу́хайте ува́жно: (Listen carefully:) [Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ь](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cJlal8XKBxo)

Think of it as a silent, helpful partner that changes the quality of the preceding consonant, making it sound significantly lighter and closer to the roof of the mouth.

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **день** | day |
| **Львів** | Lviv |

In the word **день**, the **Ь** smoothly makes the final N sound soft. In the famous city name **Львів**, the **Ь** effectively makes the L sound soft. 

### Апостроф (')

Це апо́строф. (This is an apostrophe.) The apostrophe serves the exact opposite purpose in Ukrainian writing. Апо́строф блоку́є пом'я́кшення. (The apostrophe blocks softening.) It acts as a strong blocker or a protective shield. It clearly separates a hard consonant from a following iotated vowel (**Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**). 

Why do we need this special shield? Because normally, an iotated vowel would automatically soften the consonant directly before it. The apostrophe completely prevents this softening from happening and forces the iotated vowel to stubbornly keep its full "y" sound. 

| Word | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **сім'я́** | family |
| **м'яч** | ball |

In the word **сім'я́**, the apostrophe strictly stops the **М** from becoming soft and perfectly ensures the **Я** is pronounced fully as "ya". 

> [!tip]
> Memorize this incredibly simple core rule: The soft sign (**Ь**) softens what comes BEFORE it. The apostrophe (**'**) BLOCKS softening and fiercely keeps the "y" sound in the following iotated vowel. Це пра́вило. (This is a rule.)

## Підсумок: Весь алфавіт — The Full Alphabet

Ось і все! (That is all!) Ви зна́єте весь алфаві́т! (You know the whole alphabet!) Чудо́во! (Excellent!) Congratulations on a huge achievement! You have now learned absolutely every single letter and special symbol needed to read Ukrainian. You have completely and successfully unlocked the Cyrillic code. 

Here is the complete thirty-three letter Ukrainian alphabet in its proper, traditional order:

**А - Б - В - Г - Ґ - Д - Е - Є - Ж - З - И - І - Ї - Й - К - Л - М - Н - О - П - Р - С - Т - У - Ф - Х - Ц - Ч - Ш - Щ - Ь - Ю - Я**

The entire alphabet is logically made up of ten standard vowels (which includes the four special iotated vowels), twenty-two consonants, and one special soft sign modifier. We also use two specific digraphs (ДЖ, ДЗ) and the apostrophe symbol to carefully help guide our pronunciation. 

You have confidently cracked the entire Cyrillic code today. Your next major step will be to learn about syllables and proper word division.

---

# Summary

You have successfully learned the final characters of the Ukrainian alphabet. You now completely know the affricates, the rare letters, the interesting dual nature of iotated vowels, and exactly how the soft sign and apostrophe deeply change pronunciation. You are finally ready to read absolutely any Ukrainian word you encounter!

- Який символ пом'якшує попередній приголосний? (Which symbol softens the preceding consonant?) — М'який знак (Ь) / The soft sign (Ь)
- Яка літера завжди позначає два звуки? (Which letter always makes two sounds?) — Ї / Ї
- Яка літера позначає звуки Ш та Ч разом? (Which letter represents the sounds SH and CH together?) — Щ / Щ
- Що робить апостроф? (What does the apostrophe do?) — Він блокує пом'якшення / It blocks softening

---
